lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਗੁਰ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪੂਰਾ ਨਾਨਕ ਮਨਿ ਆਸ ਪੁਜਾਏ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: dhan dhan guroo gur satigur pooraa naanak man aas pujaae |4|
lines.english: Hail, hail unto the Guru, the Guru, the Perfect True Guru, who fulfills Nanak's heart's desires. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪਾਸਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਗੋਸਟਿ ਪੂਛਾਂ ਕਰਿ ਸਾਂਝੀ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: gur satigur paasahu har gosatt poochhaan kar saanjhee har gun gaavaan |
lines.english: I seek the Lord's sermon from the Guru, the True Guru; joining with Him, I sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਸਜਣੁ ਮੇਰਾ ਮੇਲਿ ਹਰੇ ਜਿਤੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਵਾ ॥
lines.translit: gur sajan meraa mel hare jit mil har naam dhiaavaa |
lines.english: O Lord, let me meet the Guru, my best friend; meeting Him, I meditate on the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵਾ ਨਿਤ ਨਿਤ ਸਦ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਮਨੁ ਜੀਵੈ ਨਾਮੁ ਸੁਣਿ ਤੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: gun gaavaa nit nit sad har ke man jeevai naam sun teraa |
lines.english: Each and every day, forever, I sing the Lord's Praises; my mind lives by hearing Your Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਵੇਖਣ ਕਉ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਈ ਲੋਚੈ ਸੋ ਵੇਖੈ ਜਿਸੁ ਆਪਿ ਵਿਖਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: har vekhan kau sabh koee lochai so vekhai jis aap vikhaale |
lines.english: Everyone longs to see the Lord, but he alone sees Him, whom the Lord causes to see Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਤੁ ਵੇਲਾ ਵਿਸਰੈ ਮੇਰਾ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਤਿਤੁ ਵੇਲੈ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਇ ਜੀਉ ਮੇਰਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: naanak jit velaa visarai meraa suaamee tith velai mar jaae jeeo meraa |5|
lines.english: O Nanak, that moment when I forget my Lord and Master - at that moment, my soul dies. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਦਾ ਸਦਾ ਸਮਾਲੇ ਜਿਸੁ ਸਤਗੁਰੁ ਪੂਰਾ ਮੇਰਾ ਮਿਲਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: so har har naam sadaa sadaa samaale jis satagur pooraa meraa miliaa |
lines.english: He alone cherishes the Lord, Har, Har, forever and ever, who meets my Perfect True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਮੇਰਾ ਪਿਆਰਾ ਸੋ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਸਦਾ ਸਮਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: jis no nadar kare meraa piaaraa so har har sadaa samaale |
lines.english: One upon whom my Beloved bestows His Glance of Grace, cherishes the Lord, Har, Har forever.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਹਰਿ ਇਕੇ ਹੋਏ ਹਰਿ ਜਪਿ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਤੀ ਰਲਿਆ ॥੬॥੧॥੩॥
lines.translit: naanak har jan har ike hoe har jap har setee raliaa |6|1|3|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Lord's humble servant and the Lord become One; meditating on the Lord, he blends with the Lord. ||6||1||3||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Wadahans, Fifth Mehl, First House:
His Darbaar, His Court, is the most lofty and exalted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਤਿਲੁ ਤਾ ਕਾ ਮਹਲੁ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: eik til taa kaa mehal na paavai |1|
lines.english: but they cannot find even a tiny bit of His Mansion. ||1||
It has no end or limitations.
Millions, millions, tens of millions seek,
Tens of thousands of devotees worship Him in adoration.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਹਾਵੀ ਕਉਣੁ ਸੁ ਵੇਲਾ ਜਿਤੁ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਮੇਲਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: suhaavee kaun su velaa jit prabh melaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: What is that auspicious moment, when God is met? ||1||Pause||
Tens of thousands of Yogis practice Yoga.
Tens of thousands of ascetics practice austere discipline.
Tens of thousands of pleasure seekers seek pleasure. ||2||
Is there any friend who can rip apart the screen of separation?
He dwells in each and every heart, but only a few know this.
I can only make the effort, if the Lord is merciful to me.
all of my pains and doubts have been eradicated.
After wandering around for so long, I have finally come to the Saints;
I sacrifice my body and soul to Him. ||3||
Says Nanak, my God is lofty and exalted. ||4||1||
God summoned me to the Mansion of His Presence, and blessed me with the Ambrosial Nectar of His Name.
Wadahans, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਅ ਕੇ ਦਾਤੇ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਮੇਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jeea ke daate preetam prabh mere |
lines.english: You are the Giver of souls, O my Beloved God.
I am a sacrifice to the feet of the True Guru. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਸੁ ਵੇਲਾ ਜਿਤੁ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਕਰਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: dhan su velaa jit darasan karanaa |
lines.english: Blessed is that time, when the Blessed Vision of His Darshan is given;

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਜੀਵੈ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਨਾਮੁ ਚਿਤੇਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: man jeevai prabh naam chitere |1| rahaau |
lines.english: My soul lives by reflecting upon the Name of God. ||1||Pause||
True is Your Mantra, Ambrosial is the Bani of Your Word.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਇ ਨ ਜਾਵੈ ਮੇਰਾ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਅਬਿਨਾਸੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: aae na jaavai meraa prabh abinaasee |3|
lines.english: My eternal God does not come or go. ||3||
True is Your Command; You sit upon the eternal throne.
Cooling and soothing is Your Presence, all-knowing is Your gaze. ||2||
O Nanak, the Lord and Master is totally permeating and pervading everywhere. ||4||2||
You are the Merciful Master; I am Your humble servant.
By Guru's Grace, some come to understand You through the Word of the Shabad. ||1||
Your servant offers this prayer, O Beloved:
You are infinite - only a few know this.
Wadahans, Fifth Mehl:
